activities.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept and scope of Geography (branches, importance and relationship with other disciplines)
suggested_methods:
Brainstorming:
In manageable groups students brainstorm on the meaning, branches and importance of Geography, and relationship between Geography and other disciplines

Field Observation:
Guide students to observe landforms, vegetations and human activities to support the concept of geography and summarise their importance in relationship to other disciplines
activities.assessment_criteria: Basic concepts of Geography are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Relevant books, charts, and ICT simulation tools on the concept of Geography

activities.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and Earth systems
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the Earth (origin, shape, size, position and movements of the earth in the solar system, and parallels and meridians)
suggested_methods:
Brainstorming: 
Guide students to brainstorm and present about the origin, shape, size, position and movements of the earth in the solar system, and parallels and meridians

Field Observation:
Guide students to observe the Earth’s surface and identify some of the components of the solar system

Project work:
Group students to design a model on the components of the solar system (size, shape, distance, position), and then present in class
suggested_resources: Relevant books, globe, picture of solar system, relevant ICT simulation tools and models on the planet earth

activities.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.2   Demonstrate an understanding of the major features of the Earth surface
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe major features of the Earth’s surface (continents, oceans, major relief features, large inland water bodies and vegetation zones)
suggested_methods:
Questions &Answers:
Guide students to mention landforms available in their surroundings

Field observation:
Guide students to visit the nearby landforms, vegetation and water bodies available in their communities to examine the formation, distribution and their importance to the communities

Group Discussion:
Guide students to discuss the size and distribution of continents, then present in the class
activities.assessment_criteria: The major features of the Earth are correctly described
suggested_resources: Environment, relevant books, relief maps, globe/models, and relevant ICT simulation tools

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and earth systems
activities.learning_activity: (a)  Describe the internal structure of the Earth (crust, mantle, core) and their major characteristics (location, size/ thickness, density and gross composition)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the internal structure of the Earth
Group work: Assign students to design models and drawings to characterise the internal structure of the Earth
activities.assessment_criteria: The internal structure of the Earth is correctly described
suggested_resources: and flash with content on internal structure of the earth

activities.main_competence: 1.0  Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the origin and the structure of the Earth and earth systems
activities.learning_activity: (b)   Describe the main external Earth systems (Hydrosphere, Atmosphere and Biosphere)
suggested_methods: Questions &Answers: Guide students to explain the main external earth systems
Field observation: Guide students to observe and discuss the hydrosphere, atmosphere and biosphere with cases from their communities
Independent library study: Organise students to to examine the main external earth systems
activities.assessment_criteria: The external structure of the Earth is correctly described
suggested_resources: flash with content on external structure of the earth (atmosphere, hydrosphere, biosphere and cryosphere)

activities.main_competence: 2.0   Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
activities.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques of map and photograph interpretation
activities.learning_activity: (a)  Describe the basic principles of reading and interpreting maps (key, symbols, direction, margin/frame, map title, scale and contour patterns) (b) Reading and interpreting various type of maps and their importance (topographical and statistical map)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm the basic principles of reading and interpreting maps
Group Discussion: Guide students to discuss basic principles of reading and interpreting maps
Case Study: Guide students to interpret and analyse various types of maps and their importance to the community
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic principles of reading and interpretation of maps are clearly explained

activities.main_competence: 2.0   Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
activities.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques of map and photograph interpretation
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the basic concept of photograph in Geography (meaning, types, elements and uses)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm the meaning, types, elements and importance of photographs in Geography
Group Discussion: Guide students to discuss the basic uses of photographs in Geography
Guest Speaker: Invite resourceful persons (e.g., cartographer, photographer, camera man/woman) to share their experiences on uses of photographs
activities.assessment_criteria: The concept of photograph and its contents are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Relevant books and photographs (ground, vertical and oblique)

activities.main_competence: 2.0   Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
activities.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques of map and photograph interpretation
activities.learning_activity: (c)  Describe the basic principles of photograph interpretation (identifying foreground, middle ground and background, associating objects with geographical phenomena)
suggested_methods: Question & Answers: Guide students to discuss basic principles of photograph interpretation
Group Discussion: Guide students to identify different parts of photographs, interpret them and make association with geographical phenomena
Case Study: Guide students to use photographs to interpret different cases of geographical phenomena
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic principles of interpretation of photography are clearly described

activities.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between relief, climate and natural vegetation
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the major climatic regions of the world (tropical, temperate, polar and sub-tropical)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on major world climatic regions
Group Discussion: Guide students to characterise the major world climatic regions
Field visit: Guide students to observe local climate characteristics and associate them with the world climatic regions
Graphical and Maps presentation: Guide students to draw graphs and maps to characterise major world climatic regions
activities.assessment_criteria: The major climatic regions of the World are categorised or classified clearly
suggested_resources: Map, graphs, photographs, globe, variety of living organisms, and online materials on the major climatic regions of the world.

activities.main_competence: 1.0   Demonstrate mastery of the structure of the Earth
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between relief, climate and natural vegetation
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate, and natural vegetation
suggested_methods: Question & Answers: Guide students to describe the relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetation
Case study: Guide students to justify the relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetation
activities.assessment_criteria: The relationship between major features of the Earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetation are correctly described
suggested_resources: Maps, photographs, globe and online materials on the relationship between major features of the earth’s surface, climate and natural vegetations

activities.main_competence: 2.0  Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
activities.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques in land surveying
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explore the basic tenets of surveying (meaning, types, equipment, methods, and importance)
suggested_methods: Group Discussion: Guide students to explain the basic concepts of surveying
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on methods and importance of surveying
Guest speaker: Invite resourceful persons from surveying institutions/ companies to share experiences on surveying activities
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of surveying are correctly explored
suggested_resources: Relevant books on land surveying, tape measure/chain, Arrows, plumb bob, cross-staff ranging poles, pegs, notebook, pencil/pen, and relevant ICT tools

activities.main_competence: 2.0  Demonstrate mastery of basic skills and techniques in Geography
activities.specific_competence: 2.1  Demonstrate an understanding of the basic techniques in land surveying
suggested_methods: Project work: Guide students to carry out simple surveying activities around the schools and their local communities
Guest speaker: Invite resourceful persons from surveying institutions/ companies to share experiences and conduct simple surveying activities
Group Discussion: Guide students to discuss and apply the findings from surveying on socio-economic and environmental activities
activities.assessment_criteria: The simple tape/ chain surveying is correctly carried out
suggested_resources: Relevant books on land surveying, tape measure/chain, Arrows, plumb bob, cross-staff ranging poles, pegs, notebook, pencil/pen, and relevant ICT tools

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Carry out a project in Geography
activities.learning_activity: (a) Develop and carry out a simple research project in Geography
suggested_methods: Project work: Guide students in groups to prepare a good research title for the project
Field work: Guide students to conduct a simple research project in the field on geographical research titles of their choice.
activities.assessment_criteria: The research title of each group is correctly prepared. A simple research project is effectively conducted in the field by each group
suggested_resources: Environment, institutions, note books, pen, pencil, key informants, camera/smart-phones, and relevant ICT tools

activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between geography, environmental health, human settlements and economic activities
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the geographical factors that influence the spatial distribution of population, human settlements and economic activities (climate, soil, relief, vegetation, transport and water supply)
suggested_methods: Field observation: Guide students to observe and discuss on the types and characteristics of human settlements and settlement patterns from the surrounding communities
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm the factors influencing population distribution and human settlements
Case studies: Guide students to apply different cases of human settlements and link them with geographical factors
activities.assessment_criteria: The geographical factors that influence the spatial distribution of population, human settlements and economic activities are clearly described
suggested_resources: Relevant books on human settlement, population distribution, National census reports, data from birth and death rate, and migration

activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between geography, environmental health, human settlements and economic activities
activities.learning_activity: (b) Examine the impacts of population distribution on economic activities and the environment (land use and settlement patterns changes)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm about the impacts of population distribution on economic activities and the environment
Group Discussion: Guide students to discuss and present in class on the impacts of population distribution on sustainability of the environment
Field observation: Guide students to observe and summarise the relationship between population distribution and environmental change (land use change, change in settlement patterns etc)
Case studies: Guide students to apply different cases from local communities to show the relationship between population distribution and the environment
activities.assessment_criteria: The impacts of population distribution on economic activities and the environment are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Relevant books on human settlement, population, photographs on human settlement patterns environment

activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Carry out a project in Geography
activities.learning_activity: (a) Complete and submit a report of the project started in Form Three
suggested_methods: Project work: Guide students to write a report for the project started in Form Three
Role Play Organise students to practice report presentations on geographical research
Group Presentation: Organise students to present and submit a report for assessment
activities.assessment_criteria: The assessment of the project started in Form Three is completed and submitted on time
suggested_resources: Relevant books on geographical research, research reports, and relevant ICT tools
